opening:
ST. OLHA, KYIVAN PRINCESS
Troparion and Kontakion:

troparia.label: Troparion (6): Angelic powers were upon Your tomb and the guards became like dead men; Mary stood before Your
troparia.text:
tomb seeking Your most pure body. You captured Hades without being overcome by it. You met the Virgin and
granted life. O Lord risen from the dead, glory be to You!

troparia.label: Troparion (1): Raising your mind on the wings of theology, you soared far above visible creation. Seeking the God
troparia.text:
and Creator of all things and being rewarded by finding Him, you were born again to Christ in baptism. Receiving
delight from the Tree of Life, you remain incorrupt for all ages, O glorious Olga.
Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit.

kondakia.label: Kontakion (4): Let us sing hymns to the Benefactor of the world, Who exalted the God-wise Olga in the land of Rus.
kondakia.text:
May He forgive our sins by her prayers.
Now and for ever and ever: Amen.

kondakia.label: Kontakion (6): With His life-giving hand, Christ our God, the Giver of life, raised all the dead from the murky abyss
kondakia.text: and bestowed resurrection upon humanity. He is for all, the Savior, the Resurrection and the Life, and the God of all.

epistle.text:
Reading of the Epistle of St. Paul to the Romans
(Rom 15,1-7)
Brethren: We who are strong ought to put up with the failings of the weak and not to please ourselves; let each of us
please our neighbor for the good, for building up. For Christ did not please himself; but, as it is written, “The insults of
those who insult you fall upon me.” For whatever was written previously was written for our instruction, that by
endurance and by the encouragement of the scriptures we might have hope. May the God of endurance and
encouragement grant you to think in harmony with one another, in keeping with Christ Jesus, that with one accord you
may with one voice glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. Welcome one another, then, as Christ
welcomed you, for the glory of God.

verses: Verse (6): He who lives in the aid of the Most High, shall dwell under the protection of the God of heaven.

gospel.text:
(Mt 9,27-35)
At that time, as Jesus was passing by, two blind men followed Jesus, crying out, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
When he entered the house, the blind men approached him and Jesus said to them, “Do you believe that I can do this?”
“Yes, Lord,” they said to him. Then he touched their eyes and said, “Let it be done for you according to your faith.”
And their eyes were opened. Jesus warned them sternly, “See that no one knows about this.” But they went out and
spread word of him through all that land. As they were going out, a demoniac who could not speak was brought to him,
and when the demon was driven out the mute person spoke. The crowds were amazed and said, “Nothing like this has
even been seen in Israel.” But the Pharisees said, “He drives out demons by the prince of demons.” Jesus went around
to all the towns and villages, teaching in their synagogues, proclaiming the gospel of the kingdom, and curing every
disease and illness.

communionHymn: Praise the Lord from the heavens; praise Him in the highest.
